blocks.content: Shirone opened the window and looked around.
They were deep in the forest, and only the torch fixed to the coachman's seat illuminated the path ahead.
Eerie shadows of trees rushed in the opposite direction of their movement.
It was fortunate that a full moon was out, but clouds were gathering somewhere, soon covering even that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone's anxiety deepened further.
At some point, the carriage began to move slowly and then came to a halt.
The sound of the horses' snorting echoed through the stillness of the night air.
The coachman opened the door and whispered.

blocks.speaker: Coachman
blocks.content: Lady Reina, we've arrived at the rendezvous point.
Reina wrapped herself in a hooded cloak.
The others followed suit, covering their faces with hoods as they stepped out of the carriage.
The place they arrived at was a vast wasteland with a view of the horizon.
As Shirone mentally mapped the location, he figured they were somewhere on the Dawn Plateau in the northern part of Thormia.
It felt like they were being extremely thorough. It would be very difficult to attempt an assassination in a wasteland with open visibility in all directions.
Of course, if someone really wanted to, they could dig an underground tunnel, but thinking that way would lead to endless concerns.
The rendezvous point was 300 meters away from where they had arrived, and in the distance, they could see the silhouette of the escort unit from the Kazra Kingdom.
Louis, the head butler of Ogent Family, signaled them.
After Reina exchanged the signal in the same manner, the two groups finally met at the midpoint.
Louis, who approached with a torch, bowed his head to Shirone's party.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Welcome. We've been expecting you.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You've done well. I hear the preparations for entry into the capital are going smoothly.

blocks.speaker: Louis
blocks.content: Yes. We haven't detected any suspicious movements. But we can't afford to be careless. Please get in for now. I'll explain as we go.
Following Louis, the group moved forward.
A massive carriage, similar to the one Shirone had seen at Alpheas Magic Academy, was waiting for them.
The leader of the escort, named Godin, introduced himself.

blocks.speaker: Godin
blocks.content: I am Luzen Godin, tasked with escorting Prince Shirone, the first prince candidate. Given the circumstances, I understand your concerns, but there's no need to worry. The kingdom has assembled the finest escorts, so you'll reach the royal capital safely. Please, step inside.
A soldier, armed with a silver sword, opened the carriage door.
Sirone was astonished even before entering.
A glowing crystal orb was attached to the ceiling, making the interior as bright as daytime.
In addition, it was equipped with not only a bed but also facilities for having refreshments.

blocks.speaker: Godin
blocks.content: I'm sure it won't be up to your standards as esteemed individuals, but please bear with it a little longer. We'll soon escort you to the royal capital.
As soon as Shirone boarded the carriage, he lay down on the bed.
It was just like any other bed he had rested on, but the fact that it was inside a carriage made it feel strangely unfamiliar.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! Amy, this is really fun.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's called a 'house carriage.' It even has a shock-absorbing mechanism, so it can endure a journey of about 10 hours without any trouble. They say the maintenance cost is astronomical, but royalty sure is different.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: But it does mean the travel speed will be slower. It might actually be safer if we arrive quickly. Either way, we need to stay fully prepared.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I'll take the first watch. Mages can stay awake for about a day without any problems.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, let's take turns together.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's fine. You'll be busy the moment we arrive, so you should rest while you can.
Even with an escort unit present, they couldn't afford to let their guard down.
Someone had to keep watch over the group, and Amy thought she was the right person for the job.
Besides, it didn't make sense for Shirone's parents to stay up all night.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: What's this, are you ignoring me? I can handle Schema too, you know! Back when I was in my apprenticeship, I'd stay up for several nights. How about the three of us take turns instead? We don't know the situation in the capital, and having just one person tire out isn't a good idea.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy thought Reina's suggestion was reasonable, so she agreed without any objection.
Vincent and Olena asked to be included as well, but it was impossible for ordinary people to ride a carriage for 24 hours and stay up all night.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Please, get some rest. If you're already exhausted now, it'll only be more of a disadvantage later.
Vincent and Olena had no choice but to lie down on the bed.
But it seemed that fatigue was something no one could resist, as they soon fell asleep.
The remaining bed was to be shared among the three in shifts.
Reina and Amy could sleep together, but Shirone, being male, couldn't share the space with them and instead leaned against the wall to rest.
How much time had passed?
Shirone had fallen asleep while watching Reina take her turn as the lookout, but soon, Amy approached and woke him.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone, do you want me to take over if you're still tired?
Shirone opened his eyes instantly, without even a preparatory movement.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: The fact that it was his turn meant four hours had passed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: His mind was unusually clear, and he didn't feel fatigued at all.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, I'm fine. You should sleep some more. Nothing happened, right?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Yeah. It's so quiet that it's unsettling. If the soldiers were talking, we could at least hear something, but they haven't said a word, probably because they're wary of Schema.
Shirone nodded and moved to the center of the carriage.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I see. Thanks, Amy.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What's there to thank me for?
Amy crawled back to Reina's side to catch a bit more sleep.
Then, as if something suddenly came to her mind, she turned to look at Shirone while still lying down.
Shirone couldn't help but laugh at her cat-like appearance.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Well, that too…
Amy trailed off.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Ever since the emissary from the Kazra Kingdom had visited, there was something that had been weighing on her mind.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone, if you do become royalty...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You won't be learning magic anymore, will you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Well, you know. You'd become the First Prince then. Naturally, you'd have to undergo training to be a successor, and with that, you wouldn't have time to learn magic.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So that's what she'd been thinking all this time?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Come to think of it, he'd been so overwhelmed with just managing himself recently that he hadn't paid much attention to what others might be thinking.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy, I'm not going to become a prince.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What are you saying? Don't tell me you're planning to run away?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If it comes to that as a last resort, then yes, I'll have to run. But I'm going to try to persuade them so it doesn't come to that.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if they are the ones who brought me into this world, I only have two people I consider my parents. The reason I'm going to the royal capital isn't to become the successor.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's to convey my intentions. I'm going to return home and continue attending the magic academy. And….

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I will definitely keep the promise I made to you.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shirone...

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Until now, Shirone had managed to navigate life according to his convictions, and it had always worked out, but this time, the opponent was different.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: The royal family wasn't as rational as Shirone thought, nor were they easily swayed by emotions.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Royalty was an intuitive existence.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Those who held the power to sway the world only considered simple decisions of whether to act or not.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But when it reaches the level of a king, their judgment evolves so profoundly that these simple choices can advance or regress the course of history.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: To change the mindset of such a person with mere words was nearly impossible.
Even so, Amy didn't say anything.
She wanted to keep the lingering emotion from Shirone's last words trapped inside the carriage for as long as possible.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Then, later, Shirone.
Amy lay down beside Reina and drifted into a short sleep.
Reina woke everyone up with a whisper.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Wake up. It looks like we've arrived at the capital.
Amy rubbed her sleepy eyes and sat up.
She peered through the crack of the window, and it was still before dawn.
However, the carriage wasn't shaking anymore, and the sound of horses' hooves was crisp, as if they were traveling on a paved stone road.
Shirone opened the window and looked outside.
It was too dark to see clearly, but he could tell that they were passing by buildings.
They had entered the city. However, not a single house had its lights on.
A soldier on horseback approached and spoke to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Soldier
blocks.content: I apologize, but since we're conducting a nighttime patrol, you're not permitted to look outside. I'll be closing the window.
The guard closed the window with a bang.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone smacked his lips and turned around.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Tch! The moment we entered the royal capital, they suddenly changed their tone.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: We can't jump to conclusions. This is the first time that man has spoken to us. It's something we should assess carefully.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone also agreed with Reina's way of thinking.
From this point onward, it was like walking on a tightrope.
Even the smallest misunderstanding or error could put them all in danger.
By the time they entered the inner castle, dawn had broken.
One of the escort soldiers knocked on the carriage and informed them that they were now free to look around.
Shirone felt a sense of alienation in a foreign country.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It seemed that during the night, not only was it forbidden to go outside, but even peeking was restricted.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's definitely more rigid than Thormia. I'll have to be mindful all the time.
The carriage crossed a massive moat surrounding the castle and entered the inner castle.
The Kazra Royal Palace wasn't as beautiful as the buildings in Heaven, but it was enormous in scale.
As they were about halfway across the bridge, fanfare resounded.
A military band appeared, and the lower officials of the royal palace came out, clapping to welcome them.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone turned to Reina at the unexpected hospitality.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: I'm not sure. I didn't expect them to openly welcome us like this.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Given that the Kazra Kingdom grew under the protection of Terraje, Shirone's existence would undoubtedly be a thorn in the side of the Empress's faction.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: And yet, there hasn't been an assassination attempt, nor any incident.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: On top of that, this level of hospitality...

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Realistically speaking, there were only two things I could think of.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Either Orkamph was putting everything on the line regarding Shirone, or Terraje didn't consider him worth her concern.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Both the former and latter had their chances, so it was impossible to definitively say which was more likely.
She checked Shirone's expression, but he remained calm.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Even though this situation must have been more nerve-wracking for him than anyone else, his unease from the beginning had completely disappeared upon arriving at the royal palace.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: This was precisely the strength in Shirone that always impressed Reina.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: It was the same within Ogent Family. While he appeared like any other innocent boy of his age, when faced with danger, he became startlingly composed.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Well… he is now the kingdom's greatest aspiring mage.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Shirone's essence hadn't changed, but everything about him had.
After reading about him in the Spirit magazine, Reina had asked a mage she knew what an Unlocker was.
She was told it was the most profound mental state a human could achieve.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: Knowing this made it hard to see Shirone as just a child like before.
The Kazra Royal Palace spanned an area of about 20,000 pyeong (approximately 66,115 square meters) and housed roughly 1,000 residents.
Its design was more militaristic than lavish, and perhaps because of that, the entrance was narrower than other castles.
However, once inside, a massive grand hall unfolded before them, worthy of being called a royal palace.
As they passed the red carpet lined with high-ranking officials according to their ranks, they saw the throne atop the stairs, where Orkamph IV sat.
Though it wasn't proper etiquette to stare directly at the king's face, Shirone's group couldn't take their eyes off him.
He had the same blonde hair as Sirone and an elegant appearance, making it hard to believe he was the ruler of a nation.
However, the aura emanating from him was anything but ordinary.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: No matter how powerful a king may be, he was still just another person under the heavens, but there was something undeniably different about a man who held the lives of millions in his hands.
When Orkamph and Shirone were placed side by side, one could easily believe they were father and son.
Their resemblance wasn't identical, but given that they had been separated for 18 years, the differences in their upbringing had to be considered.
Shirone's group performed the ceremonial greetings as dictated by protocol.
However, Orkamph showed no reaction, focusing his gaze solely on Shirone's face.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Raise your head.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone met Orkamph's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: The king's gaze seemed to draw Shirone in, shaking him to his core.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: It was the aura of a sovereign, something that couldn't be felt from a swordsman or a mage.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: However, Shirone quickly composed himself, returning to a calm state.

blocks.speaker: Reina
blocks.content: It was the state of 'Indestructible Diamond,' where one's will is unshaken by others.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: Seeing this, Orkamph's eyes gleamed with surprise.

blocks.speaker: Orkamph
blocks.content: He truly is my son...
